items.briefing:
A new study warns that 1 in 10 Australian organisations could lose control of their systems if AI-related credentials — such as API keys or login tokens used by automated tools — are exposed. This comes alongside a separate report finding that Australian businesses are struggling to keep track of AI system identities and access permissions, leaving gaps that attackers could exploit. These risks are growing as AI tools become more common in workplaces, including training organisations.

A Chinese cyber-espionage group known as FamousSparrow has been found targeting the energy sector in a newly identified campaign, a reminder that critical industries face persistent, sophisticated threats. **Practical tip:** If your organisation uses any AI tools or software integrations, check that access keys and passwords for those tools are stored securely — never saved in shared documents or emails.
